title: trichipteris infesta
intro: Trichipteris infesta, also known as "Tiger's Claw," is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Trichipteris infesta include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Trichipteris infesta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
